text: Chronic migraines since mid-twenties, currently about two bad headaches per week, building behind the right eye without aura
transcript_quote: Since my mid-twenties. No warning lights or anything before they start, no aura, they just build behind my right eye and then I am done for the day. Lately maybe two bad ones a week.

text: Migraine associated with photophobia, phonophobia, and nausea; relieved by sleep in a dark, quiet room
transcript_quote: Dark room, no sound... Light makes it worse, and sometimes I get queasy with it. Sleep is the only thing that really resets it.

text: No change in migraine pattern, no new symptoms, no unilateral weakness, no vision changes
transcript_quote: No, same old enemy.

text: Headache today rated 2/10
transcript_quote: Like a two out of ten. Background noise.

text: Reports chronic fatigue and tiredness
transcript_quote: Tired a lot.

text: Sleeps only five to six hours; falls asleep fine but wakes early
transcript_quote: Five, six hours. I fall asleep fine, my brain just clocks in early.

text: Reports flat mood some evenings that lifts; denies hopelessness or loss of interest
transcript_quote: Not hopeless. More like flat, some evenings. It lifts.

text: Medication reconciliation completed; patient takes only abortive medication for severe headaches, no OTC/vitamins/supplements
transcript_quote: The stuff I keep for when a headache gets really bad, and that is about it.

text: Social isolation: friends scattered after college, no close social contacts outside household
transcript_quote: There is not really anyone I just hang out with anymore. I did not notice it happening. It just happened.

text: Financial stress from cut work hours; total household income ~$26,000/year for seven-person household
transcript_quote: Last year we brought in about twenty-six thousand all together, and it does not stretch.

text: Lack of reliable transportation (no car, unreliable bus) has kept patient from medical appointments
transcript_quote: We do not have a car right now... The bus is the plan, and the plan is unreliable.

text: Household unable to afford utilities when needed in past year
Domestic abuse screening (HARK) performed; total score 0, negative for abuse
transcript_quote: No. None of that. The house is chaotic, not dangerous.

text: Alcohol use minimal — about one beer per month
transcript_quote: A beer at a barbecue, maybe. Once a month?

text: Drug abuse screening (DAST-10) performed; total score 0, denies recreational drug use
transcript_quote: No. I watched what that did to a guy I grew up with. Not for me.

text: Never smoked tobacco
transcript_quote: Never. Mom was strict about it — the house was always smoke-free, and I just kept the rule.

text: Engages in high-risk activity (bike ramp jumps); counseled to wear helmet every time, wrist guards, avoid ramps taller than himself
transcript_quote: Helmet every time, wrist guards if you can, and no new ramps taller than you. Deal?

text: Blood pressure 98/83 mmHg
transcript_quote: blood pressure ninety-eight over eighty-three

text: Heart rate 93/min, likely from walking and coffee
transcript_quote: heart rate ninety-three

text: Respiratory rate 14/min
transcript_quote: breathing fourteen a minute

text: Height 176.3 cm, weight 78.7 kg, BMI ~25.3
transcript_quote: Height one seventy-six, weight seventy-eight point seven, which puts your body mass index right around twenty-five.

text: Cardiac exam: regular rhythm, no murmurs
transcript_quote: Rhythm is regular, no murmurs.

text: Lungs clear
transcript_quote: Lungs are clear.

text: Abdomen soft, non-tender
transcript_quote: Belly is soft, no tenderness.

text: Gingivitis (inflamed gums along edges) with reported gum bleeding when brushing
transcript_quote: They are inflamed along the edges — that is gingivitis. Early gum disease.

text: Referral to sliding-scale dental clinic for cleaning and gum disease care
transcript_quote: I am putting in a referral to the dental clinic that works on a sliding scale.

text: Counseled on daily oral care, especially flossing
transcript_quote: a proper dental cleaning and better daily care, flossing especially

text: Enrollment in medical ride service arranged via care coordinator to address transportation barrier
transcript_quote: our care coordinator can enroll you in the medical ride service, so the bus does not get a veto over your health care. It covers dental visits too.

text: Lipid panel: total cholesterol 198 mg/dL, triglycerides 139 mg/dL, LDL 137 mg/dL (elevated), HDL 33 mg/dL (low)
transcript_quote: Total cholesterol is one ninety-eight. Triglycerides one thirty-nine — fine. The LDL, the buildup kind, is one thirty-seven... And your HDL, the protective kind, is thirty-three

text: Dyslipidemia managed with lifestyle (more beans, fish, oats, fewer fried foods, more cardio); no medication started; plan to recheck lipids
transcript_quote: this is a food-and-movement conversation. More beans, fish, oats, fewer fried things — and the bike counts as cardio when it is not airborne. We will recheck it and watch the trend.

text: Order for repeat lipid panel (recheck) with lab slip provided
transcript_quote: the front desk will print your dental referral and the lab slip for the recheck

text: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, PF) administered in left arm
transcript_quote: You are due for a flu shot

text: Td tetanus booster (adult, preservative free) administered in left arm; was overdue
transcript_quote: your tetanus booster is overdue, which matters for a man who builds ramps out of scrap wood

text: Counseled that tetanus/vaccine injection may cause a sore arm for a day or two
transcript_quote: For a day or two. Ramp construction not recommended tomorrow.

text: Advised to keep a simple log of bad migraine days
transcript_quote: Migraines — stable pattern, keep a simple log of the bad days if you can.

text: Care coordinator to call patient this week to address stress and isolation
transcript_quote: the coordinator will call you this week

text: Referred/encouraged to attend men's group at community center Thursday evenings for social isolation
transcript_quote: there is a men's group at the community center on Thursday evenings I want you to try. Once.

text: Follow-up requested sooner than the prior two-year interval
transcript_quote: I would like to see you back sooner than two years, please.

text: Assessment of health and social care needs completed

slots.procedure: assessment of health and social care needs

text: Stress level reported as 'somewhat' on PRAPARE assessment
transcript_quote: Stress is there.

text: Currently employed full-time (household bill handler), with hours cut in spring
transcript_quote: My hours got cut in the spring.
